page_content:
Pass-Fail Grading Option

To register for a course under the pass- fail option, an undergraduate must have completed 30 or more credit hours with a GPA of at least 2.0. At least 15 of these credit hours must have been completed at University of Maryland, College Park with a University of Maryland GPA of at least 2.0.

Courses for which this option applies must be electives in the student’s program; they may not be college, major, field of concentration, or general education program requirements.

Only one course per semester may be registered under the pass-fail option.

No more than 12 semester hours of credit

may be taken under the pass-fail option during a student’s college career.

Students may not choose this option when re-registering for a course.

When registering under the pass-fail option, a course that is passed will count as hours in the student’s record but will not be computed in the grade point average. A course that is failed will appear on the student’s record and will be computed both in the overall average and the semester average.
